title: 阳光下的罪恶
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\117-阳光下的罪恶-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Weston and Cargraves discuss the logistics of the crime scene, noting that an average person could traverse the path to the beach in fifteen minutes. Cargraves details the smoking habits of various individuals, noting that Brat, Marshall, and the vicar smoked pipes, while others smoked cigarettes or cigars. They also confirm that hotel staff members, including Henry, William, and George, have alibis that would prevent them from seeing anyone approaching from the dyke path. Weston mentions a new discovery: a sandwich box found in a cave.

Kenneth Marshall visits the investigators to inquire about the funeral arrangements. Inspector Coghert returns three letters to Marshall, which the investigator uses to suggest Marshall's innocence by proving his typing speed. Weston notes that Marshall was seen typing until 10:55 and was again seen by Miss Darnley at 11:20, effectively eliminating him as a primary suspect. Shortly after, the pathologist, Nisden, arrives to report that the autopsy revealed the presence of diacetylmorphine (heroin), suggesting a drug deal is involved in the case.

Following the autopsy, Marshall walks with Rosamond Dunnley. Marshall expresses feeling the weight of public scrutiny and media misinterpretation. Rosamond advises him to face the gossip head-on, though Marshall remains passive. They discuss Poirot, with Rosamond questioning the detective's competence. Later, an agitated Linda approaches them seeking news from the police. Marshall remains vague about the investigation's progress. Rosamond eventually takes Linda aside to comfort her, suggesting that Linda should try to move past the shock, noting that Linda did not like the deceased, Eleanor.
